---
title: "Lonely Together Wrangling 1"
author: "Peter Yibo Pan"
date: "2026-03-10"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

library(tidyverse)
library(glue)
library(knitr)
theme_set(theme_minimal())
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Step 1: Import data & initial analysis
```{r data}
LTdata1 <- read_csv("Lonely Together data 1.csv")
demographics <- read_csv("Demographics.csv")
glimpse(LTdata1)

## need to select only relevant columns
LTdata2 <- LTdata1 %>% select(contains("q00")) %>%
  glimpse()
test <- is.na(LTdata2$q0001)
Ineligible 
LTdata3 <- LTdata2 %>% select( -"q0001", -"q0002", -"q0003", -"q0004")
## need to get list of column names & import into vector
q_colnames <- c("age", "gender", "gender_other") ## needs to be full length
varnames <- c(colnames(LTdata3)[1:3])
renaming <- data_frame("variables" = varnames, 
                       "names" = q_colnames )
LTdata4 <- LTdata3 %>% rename_at(
  vars(renaming$variables), ~ renaming$names)
## use vector to rename columns
```


```{r cleaning, echo=FALSE}

## separate into multiple datasets
## questions 1-4 is eligibility screening & consent

## questions 5-12 is demographics (29 total columns)
demographics <- LTdata4 %>% select(1:29)

## questions 7 gender identity and 12 racial identity needs to be condensed into single value

## questions 13, 14, and 15 are RHI (37 total columns)
RHI <- LTdata4 %>% select(30:66)
## questions 16 is DJGLS (11 total columns)
DJG <- LTdata4 %>% select(67:77)
demographics <- demographics %>% rename(ID = "Respondent ID")
no_demo <- LTdata1 %>% select(2, 44:91) %>% rename(ID= "RespondentID")

stiched_data <- demographics %>% left_join(no_demo, by = "ID")
#renaming 

library(stringr)
colnames(stiched_data) = gsub("q0013", "Peer", colnames(stiched_data))
colnames(stiched_data) = gsub("q0014", "Mentor", colnames(stiched_data))
colnames(stiched_data) = gsub("q0015", "Community", colnames(stiched_data))
colnames(stiched_data) = gsub("q0016", "Loneliness", colnames(stiched_data))
LTData5 <- stiched_data
write_csv(x = LTData5, "LTData5.csv")
## detect for incomplete surveys & missing values & extreme values (age)

LTdata5 %>% filter()
```


```{r RHI scores}
## reverse code questions Peer 8, Community 4, 7, 9, 10 


## peer, mentor, and community subscale scores 
#     RHI_Peer = mean(ALL COLUMNS WITH PEER) 
#     RHI_Peer = mean(ALL COLUMNS WITH MENTOR) 
#     RHI_Peer = mean(ALL COLUMNS WITH COMMUNITY) 
#     RHI_Sum = Sum(ALL RHI) 

## engagement, zest, and authenticity subscale scores


```



```{r DJGLS scores}
## select for positive-coding questions
## change responses 3-5 to 1 and 1-2 to 0




```
